import cv2
from PIL import Image, ImageOps

def preprocess_image(image_path, target_size=(224, 224), grayscale=False, resize=True, crop=True):
    """Preprocess an image for computer vision tasks using OpenCV and Pillow."""
    # Load image
    image = cv2.imread(image_path)
    if image is None:
        raise ValueError(f"Image at {image_path} not found or invalid.")

    # Convert to PIL Image for easier manipulation
    pil_image = Image.open(image_path).convert("RGB")

    # Grayscale if requested
    if grayscale:
        pil_image = pil_image.convert("L")

    # Resize image
    if resize:
        pil_image = pil_image.resize(target_size, Image.ANTIALIAS)

    # Crop image if requested
    if crop:
        # Center crop to target size
        width, height = target_size
        left = (width - pil_image.width) // 2
        top = (height - pil_image.height) // 2
        pil_image = pil_image.crop((left, top, left + pil_image.width, top + pil_image.height))

    # Convert back to OpenCV format
    processed_image = cv2.cvtColor(np.array(pil_image), cv2.COLOR_RGB2BGR)

    return processed_image

# Example usage
if __name__ == "__main__":
    image_path = "example.jpg"
    processed_img = preprocess_image(image_path, target_size=(224, 224), grayscale=True, resize=True, crop=True)
    cv2.imwrite("preprocessed_image.jpg", processed_img)
